model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The short-run schedule already improved optimization, so the next low-complexity step is to give the micro ResNet a bit more representational capacity by widening the feature maps and classifier head.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"insert_after","source":"","text":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),"}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After widening paid off, the next minimal capacity increase is one more residual block at the same width, which should improve feature refinement without changing the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With the model now larger, decoupled weight decay is a plausible next gain with minimal code change, and a slightly stronger decay may help the short-budget run generalize better.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples, which is a strong low-complexity way to improve short-horizon learning if runtime remains acceptable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger 128-sample batches improved the short-budget run substantially, so a higher learning rate is the simplest next step to better exploit that more stable optimization regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Now that optimization is stronger, a small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity regularization change that can improve validation loss without altering the training loop or model shape.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest recent gain came from exposing the model to more examples per fixed training step, so a moderate further batch-size increase is the most direct next test while avoiding the larger runtime jump of doubling again to 256.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger 192-sample batches improved loss again, which suggests the run can likely support a higher step size and may benefit from a more aggressive peak learning rate within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The repeated gains from larger batches suggest the run remains data-limited under the fixed 256-step budget, so moving to 256 examples per step is the most direct next test for further short-horizon improvement.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.2 + 0.8 * cosine","old":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"At the current high-batch, high-learning-rate setting, the schedule may be decaying too aggressively for such a short run, so keeping a higher learning-rate floor is a simple way to preserve optimization pressure later in the 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine","old":"        return 0.2 + 0.8 * cosine","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The higher learning-rate floor regressed validation loss, so the best next step is to revert to the previous cosine schedule shape before exploring further changes.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, lowering AdamW's second-moment timescale is a simple way to make the optimizer adapt faster to the short training horizon without changing the model or schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The shorter second-moment timescale regressed validation loss, so the next step is to revert to the previously better AdamW beta setting before trying different changes.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model is still bottlenecked to a small pooled feature vector, so widening only the lightweight classifier head is a low-risk capacity increase that adds little compute compared with making the convolutional trunk larger.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous gain came from a wider classifier head at negligible runtime cost, so pushing that lightweight capacity further is a strong next test before changing the more expensive convolutional trunk.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed, so revert to the better 128-wide head and test a small amount of dropout to regularize the now-stronger short-budget training setup.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Dropout substantially hurt this short-budget run, so the next step is to revert it and return to the previously stronger 128-wide classifier configuration.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Dropout was too strong but label smoothing has remained helpful, so the next low-risk regularization test is to reduce smoothing strength and see if it preserves generalization while allowing slightly sharper fitting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Reducing label smoothing slightly worsened validation loss, so the best final move is to revert to the stronger 0.1 setting used by the current best configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
